channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Poisonous black rain falls in Russia as Ukraine strikes oil facilities
item.dc:creator: Natalia Abbakumova, Catherine Belton
item.description: Residents of Tuapse, on the Black Sea, complained of an inadequate government response and coverup of what they say is a huge environmental and health disaster.

item.title: Rubio expected to meet with Pope Leo amid Trump’s clash with pontiff on Iran
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola, Stefano Pitrelli
item.description: The secretary of state is set to travel to Italy this week, a Vatican official said. Observers view the trip as an attempt to patch up ties with the Vatican and Italy.

item.title: Trapped in a kill zone for 177 days, his wife’s voice was his lifeline
item.dc:creator: Siobhán O'Grady, Serhii Korolchuk, Anastacia Galouchka
item.description: While Roman Mongold, a Ukrainian soldier, was pinned down and surrounded by the enemy, he managed to trade voice memos with his wife thanks to a commander’s help.

item.title: Pentagon to pull 5,000 troops from Germany, alarming Republican lawmakers
item.dc:creator: Noah Robertson, Michael Birnbaum, Adam Taylor
item.description: NATO said it was working to understand details of the plan to draw down about 5,000 troops, which coincides with a feud between the president and the German chancellor.
